reviews.review_msg: Dont understand a single word hes saying even though he speaks English. Talks in slow motion and doesnt explain concepts clearly. I found it better to just memorize and learn the concepts on my own then going to class. Leo the TA was very nice though

I don't think he understands how tough his classes and exams are. He's not a bad person, but he's a bad professor. He complicates explanations and has made lectures and tutorials mandatory while giving minimal credit for attendance. His exams and essays are overly demanding, and it seems he ignores course surveys.
do not take him, any high ratings are from the prof

somehow as unlikable as possible and makes everything way too overcomplicated. gives no time for assignments and our second midterm not a single person finished early and half the class didnt finish the exam because he gave us 4 essay q + mc + short answer like ok
honestly great prof. truely fair. there's just a lot of people who didn't put in the effort and are now complaining. put in the effort and you'll do great. concepts are not too bad.
he was a chill dude, could have grouped concepts better but overall people didnt try and now complain

honestly not as bad as people say. You have to go to class but eh also gives hints about what is on the midterms (basically the same as the practice exams tbh). I disliked how the grading is (midterm is worth 20% but only out of 20 marks so each multiple choice is 0.25???)

lectures r so dry, so boring and so content heavy. but have to go to lectures tho cuz he does in-class quizzes. he makes content harder than it needs to be and midterms were insane, worst two hours of my life. I got lucky with a good TA tho
The midterm was so badly designed. I feel like I have never seen a midterm like that. He said that he finished the whole thing in 42 min on a test run. That's egregious, and most likely not true.
expects us to finish 2 pages of multiple choice 2 pages of short answer questions and 4 essay based questions in 2 hours.

The concepts taught in this class would be more beneficial to people if it was not a writing class. The professor yaps about low averages then gives adiabolical midterm 2 on the last day of class. The difficulty of this exam was equivalent to an OCHEM 2 exam. Additionally, the prof does not respond to emails. Content seems easy prof made it hard.

I'm studying for his midterm 2 right now, and we just got our grades back for the 1 midterm which was very discouraging. Midterms were hard, and honestly too much content for the tests, not only do you have to understand the material but apply it to different cases, content very heavy. This is one of the most disliked course I have taken. Chopped.

Honestly speaking he makes this course more difficult than it needs to be; the material isn't that hard but it's the way he explains it all and how lengthy the lecture material is. Exams are difficult and honestly practice guides are not helpful considering that the exam is so much harder.
Can't say i would recommend taking this class with him

genuinely so egotistical as he makes sure to have a mandatory self glaze session each lecture then provide you with important info on assignments 24 hours before its due. heartless and rude but hes the only person who teaches hsci 319w so have fun.

The lectures and content are incredibly dry and boring and as a professor he doesn't do much to help this. The criterium provided for the assignments are incredibly vague and misleading and it doesn't help that assignments are due at noon rather than midnight for no reason. Overall, I would recommend taking this class with somebody else if possible

He is really not as bad as people say, he always gave a clear idea of what was going to be on the exams and the tests were very fair too. Lectures can be kinda dry but he explains concepts quite well and is super helpful in office hours.

had him in fall24 and he wasn't as bad as the reviews say on here. lectures do get really boring but the exams imo were fair. he gave us some of the long answer questions in advance so we could prepare which honestly helped a lot. he also gives hints on what the essay questions could be. overall not a bad professor and a really nice person.
When I spoke with him about final paper, he was not as bad as others have noted.

Lectures were long and dry, and most of them were cancelled due to the strike and his personal matters which were mostly reasonable, but he ended up throwing the slides on canvas and calling it a day. He gave lots of slides but they were difficult to understand on their own without him explaining further. That being said, his tests were very fair.

Plus there is no textbook for this class making it even harder to keep up when there is literally no lectures pretty much the entire semester. And theres pretty much no point in asking him a question his answer is pretty much always 'is that ethical though'. 
(4/8)
